rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM BREW et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 11, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is granted. The burden is shifted based on factually devoid discovery responses. (See Responses 1, 2, 4, 6, 9, 10 and 17.) Neither the Simmons nor the Newton declarations establish a basis for a reasonable trier of fact to conclude that decedent was exposed to asbestos supplied by defendant. The evidence merely establishes that defendant's product was installed on the ships as original equipment, and that insulation was disturbed when Mr. Simmons worked on the vessel. Mr. Simmons does not state that decedent was in the area when asbestos was disturbed. In addition, Mr. Simmons statement about original asbestos is speculative and is contrary to that of Mr. Ay. The parties have not explained why evidence is admissible in this case from other cases, in particular the Trueblood declaration, the Stemmer declaration and the Newton testimony. Metalclad's objections to evidence: 1. Simmons: overruled based on inconsistency; sustained for lack of foundation as to why it appeared to be original installation. 2. Stemmer: the objection based on relevance is overruled because it appears to be offered to explain the construction process as foundation for the expert's opinion. 3. Ay: the objection is overruled in part for the witness is qualified to testify as to the insulation of asbestos on Navy ships. Paragraph 6 lacks foundation as to what it is the witness reviewed. Pragraph 9 has several evidentiary problems. There does not appear to be a foundation for the statement of "the typical rate of replacement of pipe insulation on board naval vessels". The remainder of the paragraph is ambiguos at best. The only clear statement is that insulation on "ship piping can last 5 years" without it being clear what is the foundation for the opinion. 4. Cohen: the theory of re-entrainment has been criticized in Andrews v. Foster Wheeler (2006) 138 Cal.App.4t

rulings.Case Title: MACOR INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Memo Of Costs And To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 11, 2008, line 4. CROSS COMPLAINANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Strike Memo Of Costs And To Tax Costs is denied. The costs are appropriate. Given defendants' respective positions in the litigation and that this action was premised on the City's claim to the property, it is not appropriate to allocate a portion of the costs to the state. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS' et al VS. MATTHEW C KRAMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 11, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANTS MATTHEW KRAMER, STATE OF CALIFORNIA AND CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained. Hearing required on the right to amend. It is undisputed that Cal/OHSA is the agency responsible for enforcing workplace health and safety issues; that Cal/OHSA has declined to assert jurisdiction over plaintiffs' claim; and plaintiffs' have not sought administrative review of that decision. As a consequence, Labor Code 6601.5 appears to preclude review of that decision by a Court. The decisions relied on by plaintiffs - CCSO v. Dep't of Corrections (2002) 96 Cal. App. 4th 824 and Cal. State Employees' Ass'n v. Enomoto (1981) 118 Cal. App. 3d 599 - are consistent with the need for plaintiffs to complete the administrative process and seek relief by writ. Absent doing so, it would appear that res judicata and or/ collateral estoppel bars plaintiffs' claims. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: GERALD B HANSEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 11, 2008, line 9. DEFENDANT KISS, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead facts that establish a basis for a duty owed to plaintiff; simply being handicapped is not a sufficient basis. Plaintiff must also allege facts that establish a basis for the contention that plaintiff had responsibility for the area where plaintiff slipped. =(302/PJM/VC)
